messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "construction-plateau-lumpy-year1",
 "title": "Construction has plateaued while the pipeline balloons: Year-1 opens onto a digestion trough the 9% trend averages away",
 "narrative": "EMEA capacity under construction has plateaued at ~2.5 GW even as the development pipeline swelled 25% to nearly 15 GW — projects are stacking in planning, not converting to builds, because grid, permits and capex phasing bind. PDU purchase orders follow construction fit-out, not pipeline announcements. And with public cloud providers taking 61% of 2025 EMEA take-up, three or four hyperscaler capex committees synchronize the whole cycle: if 2026 is an AI-capex digestion year, CE fit-out starts pause for 2–4 quarters exactly inside the venture's window while the 2030 CAGR remains intact on paper. The 1%-of-SAM Year-1 anchor implicitly assumes an on-trend year; the conversion data says the window may be a below-trend one.",
 "category": "exogenous",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "EMEA under-construction capacity plateaued at ~2.5 GW while the pipeline grew >25% to ~15 GW — the planning-to-construction conversion, not demand, is the binding constraint",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-NL#c3",
    "digest:geo-NL#c2"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Public cloud accounts for ~61% of EMEA take-up, so a synchronized capex digestion by a handful of buyers stalls CE fit-out starts for multiple quarters; PDU orders are placed at fit-out, so Year-1 order flow decouples from the 8–10% trend consensus that the model treats as context",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-DE#c18",
    "ledger:shape.cagr"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "obtainableFactor's 1%-of-SAM anchor presumes an average demand year; a trough year roughly halves the achievable Year-1 pace without changing TAM or SAM at all",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The CAGR node is triangulated at 8–10% across three publishers and looks solid — but annual lumpiness never appears in a CAGR. The under-construction plateau statistic sits in a real-estate H2 update, not in any PDU market report, and the model has no node representing 'which year of the cycle the launch window lands in'.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Cushman & Wakefield / JLL H1-2026 EMEA prints showing under-construction capacity breaking materially above ~2.5 GW with CE construction starts rising, or hyperscaler earnings guidance re-accelerating European data-center capex for 2026.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.35,
  "rationale": "The plateau and buyer concentration are documented, but 'digestion year lands exactly in the launch window' is a timing call; CBRE simultaneously projects record 20% supply growth in Europe's five largest markets, so evidence points both ways.",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.5,
   "note": "Trough-year order flow: achievable Year-1 pace at half the on-trend anchor"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "European data-center capex commentary and guidance from the top cloud buyers",
   "where": "Microsoft/Alphabet/Amazon/Meta quarterly earnings calls, EU infrastructure line items",
   "threshold": "Two or more guiding EU DC capex flat-to-down for 2026",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "EMEA under-construction capacity metric",
   "where": "Cushman & Wakefield EMEA data centre update (half-yearly)",
   "threshold": "UC stuck at ≤2.5 GW for two consecutive prints while pipeline keeps growing",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Build a CE fit-out start-date tracker (construction starts, not announcements) to time the Year-1 sales push against real order windows",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "Converts the cycle-timing guess into an observed conversion rate"
  },
  {
   "action": "Anchor the Year-1 revenue plan on installed-base retrofit and replacement demand, which is driven by the operating stock rather than the construction cycle",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  },
  {
   "action": "Keep manufacturing and inventory commitments flexible (contract manufacturing, low fixed minimums) to survive a two-quarter order trough without cash strain",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "demand-discontinuity",
 "riskId": "risk.demand-discontinuity.construction-plateau-lumpy-year1"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
